matches.match_rationale: Inconsistent follow-up is partly a content problem — admissions staff lack ready-made, personalized outreach sequences for each inquiry source (fair vs. web vs. referral). AI text generation can produce tailored nurture email sequences, SMS templates, and follow-up scripts calibrated to inquiry type, program of interest, and days-since-inquiry. For a multi-site operator, this means each campus gets consistent, on-brand messaging without relying on individual staff to draft from scratch.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-generated library of follow-up sequences (Day 1, Day 3, Day 7, Day 14) personalized by program and inquiry channel, ready for CRM injection or manual send by admissions coordinators.

matches.match_rationale: A core pain is that not all leads are treated with equal urgency — hot leads from school fairs get lost in the same pile as cold web form submissions. Text classification can score and triage incoming inquiries by urgency, intent signals in the message, and program specificity, enabling automatic priority routing before a human ever touches the lead. This directly addresses the 'hot leads lost to competitors with faster response' mechanism.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An intake classifier that reads inquiry text (web form, email, chatbot transcript) and assigns an urgency tier and program intent label, routing high-priority leads to a fast-response queue with SLA alerts.

matches.match_rationale: Multi-site operators accumulate historical enrollment data — which leads converted, how quickly, from which source. A propensity model trained on this data can score each new lead's likelihood to enroll, enabling staff to prioritize outreach toward highest-probability prospects rather than working the list sequentially. This directly counters the 'leads forgotten or low-effort follow-up' dynamic by making prioritization data-driven.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A lead scoring model that outputs a conversion probability for each inquiry based on source, program interest, demographics, and engagement signals, surfaced in the CRM as a priority rank for the admissions team.

matches.match_rationale: The pain point is fundamentally a workflow execution failure — no standardized follow-up sequence, no CRM enforcement, leads falling through manual tracking gaps. A task automation agent can own the end-to-end follow-up pipeline: detect new lead entry, trigger timed outreach sequences, log responses, escalate unresponded leads to a supervisor, and deduplicate contacts across campuses. This is the most direct structural fix for the described pain.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An autonomous admissions follow-up agent that monitors the CRM for new leads, dispatches personalized email/SMS at preset intervals, flags non-responders for human escalation, and deduplicates cross-campus inquiries — all without manual staff coordination.

matches.match_rationale: A significant portion of lead loss happens in the first hours after inquiry when no admissions staff is available to respond. A conversational agent can engage web inquiries and school fair QR-code leads immediately — answering program questions, collecting parent contact details, and booking campus visit slots — compressing the critical response window from days to minutes. For a multi-site operator this replaces inconsistent human first-response across campuses.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A 24/7 admissions chatbot deployed on the school website and triggered via QR codes at fairs, capable of qualifying intent, answering FAQs, capturing parent details, and scheduling open-day visits — handing off a warm, enriched record to the CRM.
